back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
This shall be the law of the person with skin disease on the day of his cleansing: he shall be brought to the priest.
And the priest shall go outside the camp, and the priest shall examine, and behold, the infection of skin disease has been healed from the diseased person.
And the priest shall command that there be taken for the one being cleansed two clean live birds, and cedar wood, and scarlet yarn, and hyssop.
And the priest shall command that one bird be killed into a clay vessel over fresh water.
The live bird he shall take, together with the cedar wood, the scarlet yarn, and the hyssop, and dip them and the live bird in the blood of the slaughtered bird over the fresh water.
And he shall sprinkle the one being cleansed of the skin disease seven times, and he shall pronounce him clean, and he shall release the live bird upon the face of the field.

And the one being cleansed shall wash his clothes, and shave off all his hair, and bathe in water, and he shall be clean. And afterward he shall come into the camp and shall dwell outside his tent for seven days.

And it shall be on the seventh day he shall shave all his hair, his head and his beard and his eyebrows, and all his hair he shall shave; and he shall wash his clothes and bathe his flesh in water, and he shall be clean.
And on the eighth day he shall take two unblemished male lambs, and one unblemished ewe lamb a year old, and three-tenths of fine flour as a grain offering mixed with oil, and one log of oil.
And the priest who cleanses shall present the one being cleansed and these things before the face of the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
And the priest shall take one male lamb and present it as a guilt offering, and the log of oil, and wave them as a wave offering before the face of the Lord.
And he shall kill the lamb in the place where the sin offering and the burnt offering are killed, in the holy place, for like the sin offering the guilt offering is for the priest; it is most holy.
And the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on the right earlobe of the one being cleansed, and on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot.
And the priest shall take some of the log of oil, and pour it onto the priest's left palm.
And the priest shall dip his right finger from the oil that is on his left palm, and sprinkle from the oil with his finger seven times before the face of the Lord.

And from the remainder of the oil that is on his palm, the priest shall put it on the right earlobe of the one being cleansed, and on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot, upon the blood of the guilt offering.
And the remainder of the oil that is on the priest's palm he shall put on the head of the one being cleansed, and the priest shall make atonement for him before the face of the Lord.
And the priest shall offer the sin offering and make atonement for the one being cleansed from his uncleanness, and afterward he shall kill the burnt offering.
And the priest shall offer the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar, and the priest shall make atonement for him, and he shall be clean.

And if he is poor and his hand does not reach, then he shall take one male lamb for a guilt offering to be waved, to make atonement for him; and one-tenth of fine flour mixed in oil for a grain offering, and a log of oil.
And two turtledoves or two young pigeons, whichever his hand can reach—one shall be a sin offering and one a burnt offering.
And he shall bring them on the eighth day for his cleansing to the priest, to the entrance of the tent of meeting, before the face of the Lord.
And the priest shall take the lamb of the guilt offering and the log of oil, and the priest shall wave them as a wave offering before the face of the Lord.

And he shall kill the lamb of the guilt offering, and the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on the right earlobe of the one being cleansed, and on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot.
And from the oil, the priest shall pour onto the priest's left palm.
And the priest shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is on his left palm seven times before the face of the Lord.

And the priest shall put some of the oil that is on his palm on the right earlobe of the one being cleansed, and on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot, on the place of the blood of the guilt offering.
And the remainder of the oil that is on the priest's palm he shall put on the head of the one being cleansed, to make atonement for him before the face of the Lord.
And he shall offer one of the turtledoves or of the young pigeons, of whichever his hand can reach.
Whichever his hand reaches—one as a sin offering and one as a burnt offering—with the grain offering. And the priest shall make atonement for the one being cleansed before the face of the Lord.
This is the law of one in whom is an infection of skin disease, whose hand cannot reach in his cleansing.
And the Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
When you enter the land of Canaan that I am giving you as a possession, and I put an infection of skin disease in a house in the land of your possession—
Then the one to whom the house belongs shall come and tell the priest, saying: Something like an infection has appeared to me in the house.

And the priest shall command that they empty the house before the priest comes to see the infection, that all that is in the house may not become unclean. And afterward the priest shall come to see the house.
And he shall examine the infection, and behold, the infection is on the walls of the house—greenish or reddish depressions—and their appearance is lower than the wall.
And the priest shall go out of the house to the entrance of the house, and he shall close up the house for seven days.
And the priest shall return on the seventh day and examine, and behold, the infection has spread on the walls of the house.
And the priest shall command that they pull out the stones in which is the infection, and they shall throw them outside the city to an unclean place.
And the house he shall have scraped from the house all around, and they shall pour out the plaster that they scraped to outside the city, to an unclean place.
And they shall take other stones and bring them to replace the stones, and other plaster he shall take and plaster the house.
And if the infection returns and breaks out in the house after he has pulled out the stones, and after he has scraped the house, and after he has plastered—
Then the priest shall come and examine, and behold, the infection has spread in the house—it is a destructive skin disease in the house; it is unclean.
And he shall tear down the house—its stones and its timbers and all the plaster of the house—and he shall take them outside the city to an unclean place.
And the one who enters the house all the days he has closed it shall be unclean until evening.
And the one who sleeps in the house shall wash his clothes; and the one who eats in the house shall wash his clothes.

And if the priest indeed comes, and examines, and behold, the infection has not spread in the house after the house has been plastered, then the priest shall pronounce the house clean, for the infection has been healed.
And he shall take to purify the house two birds, and cedar wood, and scarlet yarn, and hyssop.
And he shall kill one bird into a clay vessel over fresh water.

And he shall take the cedar wood and the hyssop and the scarlet yarn and the live bird, and dip them in the blood of the slaughtered bird and in the fresh water, and sprinkle on the house seven times.
And he shall cleanse the house with the blood of the bird, and in the fresh water, and in the live bird, and in the cedar wood, and in the hyssop, and in the scarlet yarn.
And he shall release the live bird outside the city, to the face of the field, and shall make atonement for the house, and it shall be clean.
This is the law for every infection of skin disease and for the scaly outbreak.
And for skin disease of the garment and of the house.
And for the swelling and for the rash and for the bright spot.
To teach in the day of the unclean and in the day of the clean. This is the law of skin disease.
